title: X-Men #4
keyDetails: First appearances of Quicksilver, Scarlet Witch, Toad, and the Brotherhood of Evil Mutants. Second appearance of Magneto.
credits: Stan Lee story. Jack Kirby and Paul Reinman cover and art.
presentation: A major early Silver Age X-Men key with vivid red and yellow cover color, strong character presentation, and attractive overall eye appeal for the assigned grade.

images.alt: X-Men #4 CGC 5.5 front cover and CGC label
X-Men #4 CGC 5.5 back cover and CGC label

title: X-Men #9
keyDetails: First meeting of the X-Men and the Avengers. First appearance of Lucifer. Marvel Girl pin-up.
credits: Stan Lee story. Jack Kirby and Chic Stone cover and art.
presentation: A sharp mid-high-grade example with strong color, clean registration, and excellent overall presentation. The full slab and CGC label are clearly shown in the gallery.

images.alt: X-Men #9 CGC 7.5 front cover and CGC label
X-Men #9 CGC 7.5 back cover and CGC label

title: X-Men #8
keyDetails: First appearance of Unus the Untouchable. Mastermind appearance. Beast pin-up.
credits: Stan Lee story. Jack Kirby and Chic Stone cover and art.
presentation: A clean early-series copy with strong cover color, balanced presentation, and clear front-and-back photography for close inspection.

X-Men #8 CGC 7.0 back cover and CGC label

title: X-Men #11
keyDetails: First appearance of the Stranger. Magneto and the Brotherhood of Evil Mutants appearance.
credits: Stan Lee story. Jack Kirby cover. Jack Kirby and Chic Stone art.
presentation: A desirable white-page early X-Men issue with a dark, dramatic cover and strong overall eye appeal for the assigned grade.

images.alt: X-Men #11 CGC 7.5 front cover and CGC label
X-Men #11 CGC 7.5 back cover and CGC label

title: X-Men #16
keyDetails: Third appearance of the Sentinels. Third use of the bold “Marvel Comics Group” cover logo.
credits: Stan Lee story. Werner Roth and Dick Ayers art. Jack Kirby cover and layouts.
presentation: A high-grade Silver Age example with vivid yellow cover color, sharp registration, and a commanding Sentinel cover.

X-Men #16 CGC 8.5 back cover and CGC label

title: X-Men #20
keyDetails: Lucifer, Blob, and Unus appearances. Reveals how Professor X lost the use of his legs. Letter from Frank Brunner.
credits: Roy Thomas story. Werner Roth and Dick Ayers art. Jack Kirby, Werner Roth, and Dick Ayers cover.
presentation: A striking high-grade copy with strong red cover color and excellent visual presence, featuring a pivotal Professor X backstory.

X-Men #20 CGC 9.2 back cover and CGC label

title: X-Men #22
keyDetails: Count Nefaria, Eel, Unicorn, Porcupine, Plantman, and Scarecrow appearances.
credits: Roy Thomas story. Werner Roth and Dick Ayers art. Werner Roth cover.
presentation: A vibrant red-cover copy with excellent character detail and a strong mid-high-grade presentation.

images.alt: X-Men #22 CGC 8.5 front cover and CGC label
X-Men #22 CGC 8.5 back cover and CGC label

title: X-Men #39
keyDetails: The X-Men receive new costumes. Mastermind, Blob, Unus, Vanisher, Changeling, and Banshee appearances. Death of Mutant-Master. Origin of Cyclops.
credits: Roy Thomas story. Don Heck and Vince Colletta art. George Tuska cover.
presentation: A high-grade white-page copy of the landmark new-costumes issue, with a deep black cover and bold, exceptionally attractive color contrast.
